data.title: Novo Nordisk (NVO) Partners with OpenAI — Is Eli Lilly the Real Target?
data.description:
Denmark-based Novo Nordisk (NVO) is expanding beyond blockbuster drugs like Wegovy with a new partnership with OpenAI. The collaboration aims to use AI to speed up drug discovery and development, signaling a broader push by the pharma giant to tap advanced technology for its next phase of growth. However, there may be more to this move. Novo has been losing ground to rival Eli Lilly (LLY), and this AI partnership could be part of its strategy to regain a competitive edge.

For context, Novo is a pharmaceutical company focused on diabetes, obesity, and rare disease treatments, best known for drugs like Wegovy and Ozempic.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: "CoreWeave Is Becoming the Neocloud of Choice": CRWV Stock Soars on Praises for Anthropic Deal
data.description:
CoreWeave's (CRWV) shares extended their 10.87% rally on Friday with more than a 10% gain on Monday afternoon. This came as more analysts hailed its multi-year partnership with AI startup Anthropic, with DA Davidson analyst Alex Platt noting that the agreement is an additional positive indication that "CoreWeave is becoming the neocloud of choice" for leading AI companies.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Trade CRWV with leverage

DA Davidson Hikes CRWV Price Target by 40%

The deal was announced a day after CoreWeave similarly pulled back the curtain on its expanded $21 billion AI deal with tech giant Meta (META). CoreWeave said the Anthropic deal means it now has nine of the 10 major AI model developers on its list.

Platt is bullish on the deal, noting that the top AI companies are responsible for most of the rapidly growing compute demand — a trend the analyst sees persisting into the foreseeable future. The analyst called CRWV stock a Buy and raised his price target by 40%, from $125 to $175, implying almost 60% upside.

Platt is a five-star analyst who ranks 416 out of the more than 12,000 analysts tracked on TipRanks. He boasts a success rate of 88% and has an impressive 100% average return.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Nike's (NKE) Delayed Turnaround Fuels the Bear Case. The Clock Is Ticking
data.description:
Nike (NKE) now faces a timing problem, not just a turnaround one, driving the bear case as its stock has plunged over 30% in 2026. Weak guidance and limited visibility from the latest Q3 earnings report have soured sentiment on growth and margins. What could have been a necessary reset now looks like a prolonged transition for this leading global sportswear and athletic brand. Until a clear inflection emerges, NKE, despite trading at seemingly deep-value levels, risks becoming a value trap. This leads me to maintain a bearish stance on the stock.

data.title: SK Hynix Hits Record Highs on AI Strength and SanDisk Stock Index Inclusion
data.description:
South Korea-based SK Hynix jumped over 7% to fresh all-time highs on Tuesday as strong optimism around AI-driven demand lifted global chip stocks. Adding to the positive sentiment, SanDisk (SNDK) is set to join the Nasdaq-100 (NDX), further boosting confidence in the memory and storage sector.
Notably, SNDK will join the Nasdaq-100 before the market opens on April 20, replacing Atlassian (TEAM).

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

New trading tool for NVDA bears

For context, SK Hynix is a leading global chipmaker specializing in memory products like DRAM and NAND, widely used in data centers, smartphones, and AI systems. Its performance is closely tied to U.S. tech stocks, as it supplies key components to Apple (AAPL), Nvidia (NVDA), and more, making it a key beneficiary of AI-driven demand.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Why SanDisk Stock (SNDK) Is on a Tear — And Why Wall Street Keeps Raising the Bar
data.description:
SanDisk (SNDK), the data storage company, surged nearly 12% on Monday on strong AI-driven demand. The stock extended its rally in after-hours trading, supported by its upcoming inclusion in the Nasdaq-100 (NDX). SanDisk will join the index before the market opens on April 20, replacing Atlassian (TEAM), a move likely to drive fresh institutional buying.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

New trading tool for SNDK bulls

At the same time, a new wave of bullish analyst calls added to the momentum. Citigroup raised its price target to $980 from $875, while Evercore initiated coverage with a Buy rating and a $1,200 price target.

Wall Street Remains Upbeat About SanDisk

Wall Street is becoming increasingly bullish on SanDisk, with analysts raising their expectations even after the stock's sharp run. Notably, SNDK stock has gained 2,740% over the past year and 300% year-to-date.

Leading that optimism, top Evercore analyst Amit Daryanani said SanDisk is well placed in one of the most attractive parts of the AI infrastructure space—data storage—where demand is rising fast and supply is expected to remain tight for years. The 5-star analyst pointed to improving pricing, stronger margins, and growing demand from cloud and data center customers as key drivers. Over time, Evercore expects SanDisk to report stronger earnings as the market better recognizes its role in AI.

Adding to the positive sentiment, another top analyst, Asiya Merchant of Citigroup, said that demand for storage remains strong while supply stays tight, which is helping support higher prices. Merchant also noted that rising AI use is driving a surge in data creation, increasing the need for memory.

Is SNDK a Good Stock to Buy?

Wall Street analysts currently rate SNDK stock a Strong Buy, with 12 Buys and three Holds issued over the past three months. After the recent rally, the shares are now trading above SanDisk's average price target of $842.3, implying a downside of 12% from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Cathie Wood Sells Strata Critical Medical (SRTA), but Top Analysts See 120% Upside in This Penny Stock
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) made one notable portfolio move on Monday, April 13. The ace hedge fund manager continued to cut exposure to Strata Critical Medical (SRTA), a healthcare logistics company focused on organ transplant transport services. The penny stock has gained 51% over the past year and closed 2% higher on Monday at $4.12 per share, while top analysts still see more than 120% upside from current levels.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

According to daily disclosures, ARK sold a total of 182,767 SRTA shares through its ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF (ARKQ) and ARK Space Exploration & Innovation ETF (ARKX), for a combined value of about $738,378.

The sale adds to a broader trend. ARK has been steadily reducing its position in SRTA over the past week, including a sale of more than 75,000 shares on April 10. The consistent selling of SRTA indicates that ARK may be reallocating capital toward higher-conviction growth plays within the genomics and robotics sectors.

Top Analysts Remain Upbeat about SRTA's Growth Potential

Despite Cathie Wood's reducing her stake, Wall Street analysts remain positive on the stock. Leading firms believe the market is currently underappreciating Strata's unique position in the U.S. organ transplant market.

Recently, top B. Riley analyst Yuan Zhi initiated coverage of Strata Critical Medical with a Buy rating and an $8 price target. Zhi said the company operates one of the largest air transport and surgical service networks for transplant hospitals in the U.S. He noted that Strata is the only end-to-end platform in organ transplant logistics and is well-placed to benefit from long-term growth in the transplant market.

Similarly, 5-star analyst Bill Bonello of Craig-Hallum also initiated coverage with a Buy rating and the same $8 target. He said the company has made major changes over the past year, but its growth outlook is still not fully reflected in the stock. Bonello expects the valuation to improve as investors gain confidence in the company's growth and see current estimates as conservative.

What Is the Future Outlook for SRTA?

On TipRanks, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Strata Critical Medical stock based on three unanimous Buy recommendations. Based on SRTA's average price target of $9.08, top analysts see over 120% upside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Bloom Energy Stock (BE) Spikes 15% Today on Expanded Deal with Oracle
data.description:
Bloom Energy (BE) stock rose about 6% on Monday and moved higher in after-hours trading as the company announced an expanded deal with Oracle (ORCL) to support its AI and cloud operations. Oracle stock also surged about 13% on Monday and gained another 2% in after-hours trading.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade BE

Under the agreement, Bloom will provide fuel cell systems to power Oracle's growing AI and cloud infrastructure.

For context, Bloom Energy makes fuel cell systems that generate on-site power for data centers and large businesses. Meanwhile, Oracle is a cloud and software company that provides infrastructure and services to enterprises worldwide.

Expanded Oracle Deal Supports Growth

According to the terms of the deal, Oracle has committed to an initial 1.2 gigawatts of capacity, with deployment already underway across projects in the U.S. and continuing into next year. The deal also includes an option to expand up to 2.8 gigawatts, pointing to further growth ahead.

The deal builds on an existing partnership between the two companies. In a previous project, Bloom delivered a fuel cell system to Oracle in just 55 days, faster than the expected 90-day timeline.

Bloom said its systems are designed to handle the heavy power needs of AI data centers and can be installed more quickly than traditional power solutions.

BE or ORCL: Which Is the Better Stock, According to Analysts?

Using TipRanks' Comparison Tool, we compared Bloom Energy and Oracle. Analysts clearly favor Oracle, which carries a Strong Buy rating and offers about 57% upside, while Bloom Energy has a Moderate Buy rating and points to downside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: ADBE, ADSK, VEEV: Michael Burry Loads Up on Beaten-Down Software Stocks
data.description:
Michael Burry sees an opportunity in beaten-down software stocks.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The prominent investor, whose bet against the subprime mortgage market was chronicled in the book and film The Big Short, continues to make contrarian moves in the stock market. In his latest substack post, Burry highlights that he has been buying unloved software stocks as their share prices have fallen.

Burry notes that he has taken positions in Autodesk (ADSK) and Veeva Systems (VEEV), and added to an existing holding in Adobe (ADBE). Each of the three stocks is down more than 20% this year as fears of disruption from artificial intelligence (AI) has led investors to dump software names.

Burry's Contrarian Moves

In his substack newsletter, called "Cassandra Unchained," Burry writes that he sees an excellent opportunity to buy high-quality software stocks as the market has punished the group collectively since the start of the year, stressing that the current selloff is overdone.

"The credit side for software is not large enough to continue driving these stocks lower for much longer," wrote Burry. The investor previously highlighted his position in ADBE stock, saying that fears of AI disruptions had become irrational and pushed valuations down to more attractive levels.

Comparing Software Stocks

Below is a chart comparing the three software stocks purchased by investor Michael Burry. As one can see, each stock has upside potential exceeding 30%, which reflects now much each security has been beaten down this year.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Dell and HP Stocks Surge as Nvidia (NVDA) Acquisition Rumors Surface
data.description:
Shares of Dell (DELL) and HP Inc. (HPQ) jumped after a report suggested that chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) could be planning a major acquisition that might change the PC industry. According to SemiAccurate, Nvidia has been in talks for more than a year to buy a "large company," although no specific name was mentioned. Because of this, investors quickly turned their attention to major PC makers like Dell and HP.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Trade NVDA with leverage

According to Gartner, HP holds about a 19% global market share, second only to Lenovo (LNVGF) at nearly 27%, while Dell has around 17%. Due to their size, they are among the most likely companies to be involved if Nvidia wants to expand further into hardware. It is worth noting that Nvidia is already heavily investing in AI and has spent about $70 billion on partners and customers in the fiscal year ending in January to support AI growth.

At the same time, Dell is directly benefiting from this trend through its AI server business, which uses Nvidia chips and is expected to generate about $50 billion in revenue by Fiscal 2027. Because of this existing relationship, any potential deal could have a bigger impact across the industry. Following the report, Dell shares rose as much as 7.6% before pulling back slightly, while HP climbed as much as 6.3%.

Which PC Stock Is the Better Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the two PC stocks mentioned above, analysts think that DELL stock has more room to fall than HPQ. In fact, DELL's price target of $177.69 per share implies 5.3% downside versus HPQ's 2.3% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Rebound on U.S.-Iran Peace Hopes as Wall Street Grows Bullish
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed higher on Monday as optimism surrounding a U.S.-Iran peace deal outweighed unsuccessful talks between the two sides over the weekend.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

New trading tool for QQQ bulls

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • U.S.-Iran negotiations in Islamabad failed to yield a resolution to the war, with disagreements centering on Iran's enriched uranium stockpile, the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, and the release of frozen Iranian funds.
  • President Trump's blockade of Hormuz took effect at 10 a.m. Eastern Time. The move will only affect vessels entering and exiting from Iranian ports and is meant to cut off Iran's oil exports, a key source of its revenue.
  • Trump said Iran contacted the U.S. this morning with the intention of reaching a deal. "We've been called this morning by the right people, the appropriate people, and they want to work a deal," he said.
  • Both JPMorgan and Morgan Stanley issued bullish calls. JPMorgan urged long-term investors to view further volatility as an opportunity to buy the dip, while Morgan Stanley believes that the S&P 500 (SPX) correction is "largely over."
  • Dell (DELL) and HP (HPQ) closed higher on speculation that Nvidia (NVDA) is planning a major acquisition.
  • Oracle (ORCL) surged alongside bullish analyst coverage and a rally in software stocks.
  • Goldman Sachs (GS) faced pressure after its fixed income revenue fell 10% and missed estimates.
  • Other major investment firms will report their earnings later this week, including BlackRock (BLK), JPMorgan (JPM), and Citi (C).

Today's Best-Performing Sector 

Information technology was the best-performing sector on Monday, driven by a recovery in the stock market and the return of risk-on sentiment. Software stocks posted the strongest returns within the sector and staged a partial recovery from a selloff driven by the release of Anthropic's Claude Mythos last week.

Several stocks led the information technology sector's gains, including:

  • Oracle (ORCL)
  • SanDisk (SNDK)
  • Fair Isaac (FICO)
  • Cadence Design Systems (CDNS)
  • ServiceNow (NOW)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, the utilities sector faced the largest drawdown amid a rotation into tech and financial stocks and shifting investor sentiment. Utility stocks generally perform well in response to market uncertainty. With rising hopes of a U.S.-Iran deal, demand for defensive positioning eased, weighing on the sector.

Notable utilities stocks trading lower include:

  • PG&E (PCG)
  • Edison International (EIX)
  • Sempra (SRE)
  • Public Service Enterprise Group (PEG)
  • American Water Works (AWK)
data.author: Eddie Pan
